title: centaurea × turolensis
intro: Centaurea × turolensis, also known as Mountain Bluet or Turolensis Cornflower, is a hybrid plant species within the Centaurea genus, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Centaurea × turolensis are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
